title: SCHC: Integer overflow in CoAP option parsing causes incorrect OSCORE detection
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/schc/schc.c
Lines: 350-360 (delta), 368-378 (length)

In coap_has_oscore_option(), the delta and length variables are declared as uint8_t, but CoAP extended option delta/length values can exceed 255:

1. When delta==13: delta = coap[offset] + 13; can be up to 268 (255+13), truncating to 12
2. When delta==14: delta = read_be16(...) + 269; can be up to 65804, truncating to just the low 8 bits
3. Same issues for the length variable at lines 368-378

Impact:
- Incorrect option number accumulation (option_number += delta uses truncated value)
- Wrong offset advancement (offset += length uses truncated value)
- Could parse into middle of option values as if they were headers
- False positive/negative OSCORE detection causing wrong compression rule selection
- Packets compressed with rule 0/1 instead of 5/6 (or vice versa) will fail to decompress

Fix: Change delta and length from uint8_t to uint16_t (or size_t for length since it's used in offset arithmetic).

Code:
```c
uint8_t delta = (byte >> 4) & 0x0F;  // Should be uint16_t
uint8_t length = byte & 0x0F;        // Should be uint16_t or size_t
...
delta = read_be16(&coap[offset]) + 269;  // Truncates to 8 bits!
```
close_reason: Fixed: Changed delta from uint8_t to uint32_t and length from uint8_t to size_t in coap_has_oscore_option() to prevent integer overflow when parsing extended CoAP option delta/length values (up to 65804). Added SECURITY comment explaining the rationale.
